Replace @jmondi/oauth2-server with @better-auth/oauth-provider (#449)

* Replace @jmondi/oauth2-server with @better-auth/oauth-provider

Consolidates OAuth2 provider functionality into better-auth by replacing
the @jmondi/oauth2-server library with the @better-auth/oauth-provider
plugin, removing ~10 repository/service files and aligning token
validation with the existing better-auth JWT infrastructure.

Key changes:
- Add oauthProvider and jwt plugins to better-auth config with custom
  accessTokenClaims that embed actorId for multi-actor support
- Migrate OAuth tables to plugin schema (oauthClient, oauthAccessToken,
  oauthRefreshToken, oauthConsent) via new migration
- Rewrite createApplication to write directly to oauthClient with
  SHA-256 base64url secret hashing matching better-auth's defaultHasher
- Update OAuthGuard to verify JWT via verifyAccessToken from
  better-auth/oauth2 using JWKS endpoint
- Proxy /oauth/token and /oauth/revoke through better-auth handler;
  redirect /oauth/authorize to /api/auth/oauth2/authorize
- Update consent flow in AuthorizeCard to POST to /api/auth/oauth2/consent
- Remove @jmondi/oauth2-server, jsonwebtoken, peggy dependencies

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove peggy dependency from signature.ts

Replace the peggy-based HTTP Signature header parser with a simple regex
implementation. The grammar was trivially equivalent to /([a-zA-Z0-9]+)="([^"]*)"/g,
which parses the comma-separated key="value" pairs in RFC 7230 signatures
without requiring an external parser generator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review issues in OAuth provider migration

- Fix migration: use `uri` column (not `website`) and hash secrets with SHA-256/base64url when migrating existing clients
- Fix migration: add unique constraint to oauthRefreshToken.token
- Fix OAuthGuard: return 500 for infrastructure errors instead of 401
- Fix token route: safe JSON parse with content-type check; map unknown status codes to 500
- Fix AuthorizeCard: add `state` to server_error redirect; route deny flow through better-auth consent endpoint; wrap URL construction in try/catch
- Fix revoke route: check and log non-2xx responses from better-auth
- Fix createApplication: use rejection sampling to eliminate modulo bias in random string generation
- Fix wellknown issuer: remove trailing slash to match JWT `iss` claim

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bind OAuth access tokens to the selected actor, not the default

Use better-auth's postLogin.consentReferenceId to capture the session's
actorId at consent time (when the user has already switched to their
desired actor). This referenceId flows through the authorization code
to the token, where customAccessTokenClaims reads it and embeds it as
the actorId JWT claim.

Previously, tokens always carried account.defaultActorId regardless of
which actor the user selected on the consent screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address second round of PR review issues

- Migration: guard null client.secret before hashing (nullable in old schema)
- createApplication: always create new client instead of silently rotating
  secret on re-registration, which broke existing Mastodon clients
- OAuthGuard: check token revocation by looking up in oauthAccessToken table,
  so revoked tokens are rejected immediately instead of lasting until JWT expiry
- Actor switch: update better-auth session's actorId in DB so consentReferenceId
  picks up the selected actor when minting OAuth access tokens
- Revoke route: propagate 5xx errors from better-auth instead of always 200
- AuthorizeCard: handleDeny now sets isSubmitting to prevent double-submit;
  extract shared buildOAuthQuery/redirectWithError helpers to remove duplication
- jest.config.mjs: add @better-auth to transformIgnorePatterns
- /api/oauth/authorize: respect ACTIVITIES_INSECURE_AUTH for scheme selection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix token error responses and well-known scheme selection

- Token route: only append created_at on success responses; error bodies
  (invalid_grant etc.) are returned as-is per RFC 6749 §5.2
- Well-known metadata: use ACTIVITIES_INSECURE_AUTH flag for scheme
  selection so issuer/endpoints match the http:// baseURL used by
  auth.ts and OAuthGuard.ts in local dev

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address OAuth PR review comments: POST body handling, error responses, and UX

- Fix POST /api/oauth/authorize to merge form body params before redirect
- Treat all verifyAccessToken failures as 401 (not only named errors)
- Trim and filter redirect URIs in createApplication
- Disable Approve/Deny buttons while actor switch is in-flight (isSwitching)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix handleDeny finally block, OAuthGuard error handling, and migration column types

- Add finally block to handleDeny so isSubmitting resets even when redirect_uri is absent
- Split OAuthGuard catch blocks: verifyAccessToken failures → 401, infra errors → 500
- Replace jsonb with text in OAuth migration to comply with AGENTS.md SQL guidelines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix hardcoded https in authorize page, add session update error handling

- Fix ACTIVITIES_INSECURE_AUTH not respected in signin redirect URL
- Wrap session actorId DB update in try/catch with explicit 500 + log
- Add comment explaining shouldRedirect: false in postLogin config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Validate redirect URIs non-empty, fix host scheme detection

- Return error if all redirect_uris are whitespace-only after trim/filter
- Replace startsWith('http') with includes('://') to avoid false positive
  on hostnames like httpbin.org that begin with 'http'

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: error handling, open redirect, unused param, revoked token test

- Add try/catch + console.error to handleActorChange and handleApprove in AuthorizeCard;
  redirectWithError on server_error fallback
- Fix open redirect in authorize page.tsx: validate redirect_uri against client.redirectUris
- Fix scheme detection: startsWith('http') → includes('://') in authorize routes
- Wrap auth.handler calls in try/catch with logger.error → 500 in token route
- Log parse errors in authorize POST body handler
- Add try/catch around getAccountFromId in auth.ts session hook
- Remove unused database: Database param from createApplication; update call sites
- Validate empty/whitespace-only redirect_uris in createApplication
- Add test for revoked token returning 401 in OAuthGuard.test.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix P0: hash bearer token before oauthAccessToken revocation lookup

better-auth stores tokens with SHA-256 base64url (storeTokens: "hashed"
default). The raw bearer token never matched the stored hash, causing every
token-authenticated request to be rejected as revoked (401).

Hash the incoming token with the same transform before the DB lookup.
Update test mock to store hashed tokens to match the new behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: extract getBaseURL, fix CORS on token error, cleanup

- Extract duplicated getBaseURL logic into shared utility in lib/config
- Fix CORS headers missing on token endpoint error path
- Remove unnecessary async from hashClientSecret
- Add destructive rollback warning to OAuth migration down

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix double-scheme URL in authorize page, validate redirect URIs

- Use getBaseURL() in authorize page instead of manual scheme construction
  to prevent double-scheme when config.host includes ://
- Validate redirect URIs are well-formed URLs before inserting into DB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle opaque tokens, remove console.error, add error handling

- OAuthGuard: fall back to opaque token DB lookup when JWT verification
  fails (e.g. Mastodon clients that don't send resource param)
- OAuthGuard: check expiration and scopes for opaque tokens manually
- OAuthGuard: extract actorId from stored referenceId for opaque tokens
- AuthorizeCard: remove console.error calls (violates project rules)
- auth.ts: wrap consentReferenceId DB call in try/catch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CORS headers on revoke endpoint error paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix scope parsing, test isolation, error codes, and token nullability

- Parse opaque token scopes as JSON array (matching better-auth adapter storage)
- Clear mockHandler in beforeEach to prevent cross-test assertion leaks
- Use server_error for infrastructure failures in handleDeny per RFC 6749
- Make oauthAccessToken.token column notNullable for data integrity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Harden OAuthGuard: JWT/opaque split, scope enforcement, token TTLs

- Detect JWT format (three dot-separated segments) before verification;
  reject failed JWTs immediately instead of falling through to opaque DB
  lookup, preventing tampered/expired JWTs from authenticating via hash
  collision with a valid DB entry
- Add defense-in-depth explicit JWT scope check after verifyAccessToken
- Reduce access token TTL to 7 days (was 30 days = same as refresh token)
- Document unbounded oauthClient table growth concern
- Add tests: JWT scope mismatch, expired/tampered JWT with valid DB row,
  opaque tokens skip JWT verification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Defensive scope parsing, realistic test data, preserve 4xx in token proxy

- parseStoredScopes: wrap JSON.parse in try/catch for malformed data
- Expired JWT test: use real actor referenceId for stronger assertion
- Token route: map unmapped 4xx to 400 instead of 500

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use safeParse for app registration, document token hashing

- Use Zod safeParse in POST /api/v1/apps to return 422 on invalid input
  instead of letting ZodError propagate as 500
- Add source comment for token hashing assumption (storeToken in
  better-auth hashes both JWT and opaque tokens via defaultHasher)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add clarifying comments for disabledPaths and redirect URI validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reject javascript:/data:/vbscript: redirect URIs explicitly

new URL() accepts these schemes, so an explicit blocklist is needed.
Corrects incorrect comment that claimed they were already rejected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix redirect_uris newline split, add jwks_uri, return db UUID as id

- Split redirect_uris by newline per Mastodon API spec (P1)
- Add jwks_uri to OAuth well-known metadata per RFC 8414 (P2)

Previously only 5xx was propagated; 4xx (e.g. 401 for invalid client

commit.message:
Add contextual Tips & Tricks section for remaining daily protein goal (#7)

Users had no in-app guidance on how to close the gap between their
current intake and daily protein goal. This adds a dynamic tips section
that surfaces food suggestions calibrated to the remaining amount.

### Changes

- **`index.html`** — New `#tips-section` container placed below Today's
Log; hidden by default via `display:none`
- **`app.js`**
  - `getTips(remaining)` — returns 3 contextual tips across 4 tiers:
    | Remaining | Suggestions |
    |-----------|-------------|
    | ≥ 60g | Chicken breast 200g, tuna + shake combo, meal planning |
    | 30–59g | Whey shake, tuna 100g, Greek yogurt + cottage cheese |
    | 10–29g | Greek yogurt 150g, two eggs, cottage cheese 100g |
    | < 10g | Milk 200ml, one egg, cottage cheese 50g |
- `renderTips()` — shows/hides section based on remaining; hidden once
goal is met
- `render()` now calls `renderTips()` so tips update live on every entry
change
- **`style.css`** — `.tip-item` uses a green left-border accent
(`border-left: 3px solid var(--green-500)`) and reuses existing
surface/text-muted variables; consistent with the log item design

commit.message:
feat: index title + og:description for richer semantic embeddings

Previously only the article title was embedded. Now each document is
indexed as 'Title. Description' using the og:description meta tag,
which contains a meaningful summary of the article content.

This significantly improves semantic search recall — queries about
concepts mentioned only in the article body (not the title) now match
correctly. Existing indexes should be rebuilt with build-index.

Also includes entity decoding for meta tag content (& etc.) and
expanded Range header from 8KB to 16KB to capture longer <head> sections.
